text.title: Tailwind CSS: Configuration and Layout Patterns
text.summary: v3 and v4 config examples, common layout patterns (container, card, stack, center), dark mode strategy, and plugin registration.

section.title: Dark Mode Strategy
section.note: Use `darkMode: 'class'` (v3) or `.dark` selector (v4) for explicit control. Use `media` only when you do not need a manual toggle. Never switch strategies after launch — it changes all component dark: selectors.

antipattern.description: Agents mix v3 tailwind.config.ts and v4 @theme block in the same project.
antipattern.reason: v4 ignores tailwind.config.ts; v3 ignores @theme. Specify the major version in every agent prompt. Check tailwindcss package version before generating config.

antipattern.description: Agents install @tailwindcss/forms but forget `strategy: 'class'`, breaking existing inputs.
antipattern.reason: Without `strategy: 'class'`, the forms plugin applies global input resets that override component styles. The strategy must match the project's existing form styling approach.

antipattern.description: Agents write `@apply` chains with 8–15 utilities in a global stylesheet.
antipattern.reason: Long @apply chains recreate the BEM stylesheet maintenance problem. Anything needing more than 3 utilities is a component, not a utility alias.

antipattern.description: After adding a new template file (Blade, Django, ERB), agents forget to update the content glob.
antipattern.reason: JIT only scans files matching content glob patterns. New template types silently produce unstyled output until the config is updated.

text.reference: Full v3 Tailwind config with extended colors, fontFamily, spacing, and plugins.
v4 CSS-first config with @theme block for brand tokens.
